response.output:
Now I have a comprehensive view of the entire E2E test suite. Let me compile the audit report.

---

## E2E Test Audit Report — VirtualGfriend

### 1. BROKEN / FRAGILE LOCATORS

#### ChatPage.ts
| Severity | Issue | Location |
|---|---|---|
| **HIGH** | `messageInput` uses `getByRole('textbox', { name: /message\|nhắn\|type\|input/i }).first()` — regex with pipe `|` is treated as literal pipe, **not alternation**. Should be `/message\|nhắn\|type\|input/i` → `/message|nhắn|type|input/i`. This will **never match** anything meaningful. | Line 57 |
| **HIGH** | Same pipe-regex bug on `sendButton`: `/send\|gửi\|send message/i` | Line 58 |
| **MEDIUM** | `backButton: page.locator('button:has(svg.lucide-arrow-left)')` — `:has()` CSS selector is **not natively supported** in all browsers and is deprecated in favor of Playwright's `.filter({ has })` pattern. Works in Chromium but fragile. | Line 44 |
| **MEDIUM** | Same `:has()` pattern used on `sceneButton`, `giftButton`, `emojiButton`, `micButton`, `sendGiftButton`, `closeGiftModal`, `searchButton` — **7 locators** relying on `button:has(svg.lucide-*)`. If icon library changes or SVGs are wrapped, all break. | Lines 44-48, 60-62 |
| **MEDIUM** | `passwordInput` in LoginPage: `'input[type="password"], input[placeholder*="••"]'` — the `•` bullet character may render differently across OS/browsers (macOS uses `●`, Linux `•`). Fragile. | LoginPage.ts Line 27 |
| **MEDIUM** | `usernameInput` in RegisterPage: `'input[name="username"], input[placeholder*="username" i]'` — the `i` flag in CSS selector is **not universally supported**. Works in Playwright/Chromium but not standard CSS. | RegisterPage.ts Line 25 |
| **LOW** | `pageTitle: page.locator('h1, [class*="CardTitle"]').first()` — too generic, could match any `h1` on page. | LoginPage.ts Line 33, RegisterPage.ts Line 30 |
| **LOW** | `characterName: page.locator('[data-testid="character-name"], h2:first-of-type')` — `h2:first-of-type` is extremely broad and will match wrong elements on pages with multiple h2s. | DashboardPage.ts Line 49 |
| **LOW** | `loadingSpinner: page.locator('[data-testid="loading-spinner"], .animate-spin')` — `.animate-spin` is a Tailwind utility class used on **any** spinning element (loading, icons, etc). Will cause false positives. | BasePage.ts Line 16 |

#### PasswordResetPages.ts
| Severity | Issue | Location |
|---|---|---|
| **MEDIUM** | `backToLoginLink: page.locator('a[href="/auth/login"], a:has-text("Quay lại")')` — `a:has-text()` is Playwright-proprietary, fine, but "Quay lại" means "Go back" and could match any back link, not specifically login. | Line 23 |
| **MEDIUM** | `otpInputs: page.locator('input[maxlength="1"]')` — fragile. If the app switches to a single OTP input field, this breaks entirely. | Line 80 |

---

### 2. MISSING ASSERTIONS (Tests That Always Pass)

| Severity | Test | Issue |
|---|---|---|
| **CRITICAL** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should not send empty message" | **Zero assertions**. Just checks `isDisabled` and does nothing with the result. Test always passes. | Line 94-102 |
| **CRITICAL** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should show typing indicator while waiting" | **Zero assertions**. Just sends a message and waits 500ms. No verification of typing indicator. | Lines 67-77 |
| **CRITICAL** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should search chat history" | Conditional `if (isSearchVisible)` — if search isn't implemented, test silently passes. No assertions at all. | Lines 118-127 |
| **CRITICAL** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should open gift modal" | Uses `if (!isModalVisible) { console.log(...) }` — **console.log is not an assertion**. Test always passes. | Lines 108-116 |
| **HIGH** | `password-reset.spec.ts` → "should show error for non-existent email" | Comment-only "Should show error or info message". No assertion. | Lines 28-34 |
| **HIGH** | `password-reset.spec.ts` → "should have resend OTP option" | All 4 OTP tests are **`.skip()`'d** — zero coverage. | Lines 60-94 |
| **HIGH** | `password-reset.spec.ts` → "should display password reset form" | `expect(count).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0)` — **always passes**, even with 0 password inputs. | Lines 98-104 |
| **HIGH** | `password-reset.spec.ts` → "should validate password requirements" | No assertion after submit. Just `waitForTimeout(1000)`. | Lines 106-114 |
| **HIGH** | `password-reset.spec.ts` → "should validate password match" | No assertion after submit. Just `waitForTimeout(1000)`. | Lines 116-125 |
| **HIGH** | `login.spec.ts` → "should show error for empty credentials" | `const hasError = await loginPage.isToastVisible();` — **never asserted**. Value is captured but not checked. | Lines 20-25 |
| **HIGH** | `login.spec.ts` → "should show loading state during login" | **Zero assertions**. Just clicks and waits 100ms. | Lines 82-91 |
| **MEDIUM** | `register.spec.ts` → "should show error for duplicate email" | Uses `expect(hasToast || onRegisterPage).toBeTruthy()` — logical OR means if the page stays on register (which it would for ANY error), test passes. Doesn't verify it's specifically a duplicate email error. | Lines 80-90 |
| **MEDIUM** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should handle long messages" | Only asserts URL still contains `/chat`. Doesn't verify the message was handled correctly, truncated, or rejected. | Lines 104-113 |

---

### 3. FLAKY TESTS (Timing / Race Conditions)

| Severity | Test | Issue |
|---|---|---|
| **CRITICAL** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should receive AI response" — `sendMessageAndWaitForResponse` | Uses `.catch(() => {})` to swallow errors on typing indicator waits (lines 86-89 in ChatPage.ts). If typing indicator never appears, test silently continues. Then asserts `toHaveCount(currentMessagesCount + 2)` — if AI responds at different speed or sends multiple messages, this fails randomly. **60s timeout** will cause test suite hangs. |
| **CRITICAL** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should send a message" | Uses `page.waitForTimeout(2000)` — hardcoded wait. On slow CI, 2s may not be enough; on fast machines, wastes time. |
| **HIGH** | All password-reset tests | Use `page.waitForTimeout(1000)`, `waitForTimeout(2000)`, `waitForTimeout(3000)` — **hardcoded sleeps** instead of proper waits. Fragile across environments. |
| **HIGH** | `login.spec.ts` → "should persist login state after page refresh" | After `page.reload()`, there's **no wait** before checking `isAuthenticated()`. localStorage may not be restored yet. |
| **HIGH** | `onboarding.spec.ts` → Multiple tests | Uses `page.waitForTimeout(5000)` after character creation. Arbitrary timeout that may not be enough on slow systems. |
| **HIGH** | `ChatPage.sendMessageAndWaitForResponse` | `.catch(() => {})` **swallows all errors** including real failures. Test can't distinguish "typing indicator didn't show" from "bot never responded". |
| **MEDIUM** | `register.spec.ts` → "should register successfully" | `waitForTimeout(3000)` before checking URL — may not be enough for full redirect + page load on slow CI. |
| **MEDIUM** | `onboarding.spec.ts` → "should go back to previous step" | Uses `page.waitForTimeout(500)` for animations — may not be enough on slower machines. |
| **MEDIUM** | `DashboardPage.waitForPageLoad` | `Promise.race([...]).catch(() => {})` — silently catches the case where neither character name nor create button appears. Test continues in broken state. |
| **MEDIUM** | `BasePage.waitForLoadingComplete` | `loadingSpinner.waitFor({ state: 'hidden', timeout: 10000 }).catch(() => {})` — if spinner never appears, waits full 10s unnecessarily every test. |

---

### 4. COVERAGE GAPS (Critical Flows Without Tests)

| Area | Missing Tests |
|---|---|
| **Gift System** | No UI tests for sending gifts, gift modal interactions, gift history. Only a skipped/weak `openGiftModal` test. |
| **Scene System** | No UI tests for scene selection, scene changing, scene-modal interactions. ChatPage has locators but no tests. |
| **Quest System** | No UI tests for viewing quests, claiming rewards, quest progress tracking. |
| **Memory/Gallery** | No tests for memory creation, viewing gallery, favoriting memories. |
| **Settings** | No UI tests for settings page (only a navigation check in dashboard). No tests for language, theme, notification toggles. |
| **Premium/Gating** | No tests for premium feature access, tier upgrades, payment flow. |
| **Mobile Responsive** | Only 1 mobile test (chat page). No mobile tests for auth, onboarding, dashboard, settings. |
| **Navigation/Router** | No tests for 404 pages, deep linking, URL params handling. |
| **Error States** | No tests for network errors, API failures displayed to user, offline mode. |
| **Accessibility** | No accessibility tests despite having `checkBasicAccessibility` helper (unused). |
| **WebSocket/Real-time** | No tests for real-time features (typing indicators, live messages, notifications). |
| **DM System** | No tests for direct messaging (schema has `ConversationMember`, `DirectMessage`). |
| **Streak/Daily Rewards** | No tests for daily login streak, daily reward claiming. |
| **Relationship System** | No tests for relationship stages, affection progression, level-up events. |
| **Full E2E User Journey** | No test that covers: Register → Onboarding → Chat → Send Gift → Complete Quest → View Memory in a single flow. |

---

### 5. PAGE OBJECT ISSUES

#### ChatPage.ts
| Issue | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Incorrect `fillInput` call** | `typeMessage` calls `this.fillInput(this.messageInput, message)` — `fillInput` is from BasePage and uses `locator.fill()`. This is correct, but `typeMessage` and `enterMessage` are **duplicate aliases** (DRY violation). |
| **Missing `waitForResponse` pattern** | `sendMessageAndWaitForResponse` relies on DOM counting instead of intercepting the actual API call. Should use `page.waitForResponse()` for the chat send endpoint. |
| **Gift button may not exist** | If user doesn't have gift feature unlocked, `openGiftModal` will fail with locator not found. No defensive check. |
| **`search()` method fills but doesn't trigger** | `search()` fills input but doesn't press Enter or click search button — incomplete interaction. |

#### BasePage.ts
| Issue | Detail |
|---|---|
| **`clickWithRetry` masks real bugs** | Manual retry loop (3 retries) is an anti-pattern. If a click needs retry, the underlying locator or timing is wrong. Should use Playwright's built-in auto-waiting instead. |
| **`isAuthenticated` is hardcoded to `vgfriend-auth` key** | If the app changes the localStorage key, this silently returns `false` and breaks all auth-dependent assertions. |
| **`closeToast` uses Sonner-specific selector** | `[data-sonner-toast]` assumes Sonner toast library. If toast library changes, this breaks. Should be abstracted. |
| **`loadingSpinner` selector too broad** | `.animate-spin` matches any Tailwind spinning animation, not just page-level loading spinners. |

#### LoginPage.ts
| Issue | Detail |
|---|---|
| **`showPasswordButton` selector fragile** | `page.locator('button[type="button"]').filter({ has: page.locator('svg') }).first()` — if form has any other button with an SVG (e.g., info icon), this grabs the wrong one. |

#### RegisterPage.ts
| Issue | Detail |
|---|---|
| **`usernameInput` visibility check in `enterUsername`** | `if (await this.usernameInput.isVisible())` — this is a sync check inside an async method. Should use `locator.isVisible()` properly. Actually it does, but it's an anti-pattern to conditionally fill — tests should explicitly test both paths. |
| **`registerButton` filter may fail** | `.filter({ hasText: /Đăng ký\|Register/i })` — same pipe regex bug as ChatPage. The `|` is a literal character, not alternation. Should be `/Đăng ký|Register/i`. |

#### OnboardingPage.ts
| Issue | Detail |
|---|---|
| **`setAge` uses `.fill()` on range input** | `input[type="range"]` does **not support `.fill()`**. Should use `.evaluate()` to set value or drag the slider. This will throw an error. |
| **`nextButton` selector too broad** | Matches any button with "Tiếp", "Next", or arrow-right SVG. Could match wrong button on multi-step forms. |

#### PasswordResetPages.ts
| Issue | Detail |
|---|---|
| **ResetPasswordPage `goto()` ignores params** | `goto(email, token)` accepts params but tests call `goto()` without them — tests navigate to bare `/auth/reset-password` which likely 404s or shows empty page. |
| **VerifyOTPPage `goto()` URL construction** | Appends `?email=` but app may expect different param format or use route params instead. |

---

### 6. CONFIGURATION & INFRASTRUCTURE ISSUES

| Severity | Issue |
|---|---|
| **HIGH** | **Duplicate fixture files**: Both `e2e/fixtures/test-fixtures.ts` and `e2e/tests/fixtures.ts` define nearly identical fixtures. Tests import from `../../fixtures` (which points to `fixtures/index.ts` → `test-fixtures.ts`), but `tests/fixtures.ts` is a stale duplicate. |
| **HIGH** | `playwright.config.ts` runs **3 browsers (chromium, firefox, webkit) + 2 mobile** — all UI tests run 5×. With flaky tests, this amplifies failure noise. No tag-based filtering configured. |
| **HIGH** | `retries: process.env.CI ? 2 : 1` — retries in non-CI hide flaky tests during development. Should be 0 locally. |
| **MEDIUM** | `global-setup.ts` creates test user but **never cleans up**. Over time, database accumulates test data. `global-teardown.ts` is empty. |
| **MEDIUM** | API tests use a single `timestamp` variable across all tests in a file. If test file runs slowly, some tests could have stale timestamps affecting unique email generation. |
| **MEDIUM** | No `test.skip` for known-broken features. Tests like "should receive AI response" will hang 60s if AI service is down. Should be conditionally skipped. |
| **LOW** | `webServer` config starts both client and server dev servers. In CI, this is very slow. Should use pre-built production servers. |

---

### Summary Metrics

| Category | Count |
|---|---|
| Broken/Fragile Locators | **13** |
| Missing/Empty Assertions | **13** |
| Flaky/Timing Issues | **11** |
| Coverage Gaps (missing flows) | **15** |
| Page Object Defects | **11** |
| Config/Infrastructure Issues | **7** |
| **Total Issues Found** | **70** |

---

### Recommendations (Priority Order)

1. **Fix regex pipe bug** in ChatPage (`messageInput`, `sendButton`) and RegisterPage (`registerButton`) — these selectors are fundamentally broken.
2. **Add assertions** to the 6 tests with zero assertions — they provide no value and create false confidence.
3. **Replace `.catch(() => {})` error swallowing** in ChatPage with proper error handling — tests should fail loudly.
4. **Replace `waitForTimeout()` with proper waits** — use `expect().toBeVisible()`, `waitForResponse()`, `waitForURL()` instead of arbitrary sleeps.
5. **Fix `setAge` in OnboardingPage** — `.fill()` doesn't work on range inputs.
6. **Fix ResetPasswordPage tests** — they navigate to bare URL without required token params.
7. **Add critical E2E flow tests**: gift sending, quest claiming, settings, full user journey.
8. **Remove duplicate fixtures file** (`tests/fixtures.ts`).
9. **Set retries to 0 locally** to surface flaky tests during development.
10. **Add cleanup to global-teardown.ts** to prevent database pollution.

resultDisplay.result:
Now I have a comprehensive view of the entire E2E test suite. Let me compile the audit report.

---

## E2E Test Audit Report — VirtualGfriend

### 1. BROKEN / FRAGILE LOCATORS

#### ChatPage.ts
| Severity | Issue | Location |
|---|---|---|
| **HIGH** | `messageInput` uses `getByRole('textbox', { name: /message\|nhắn\|type\|input/i }).first()` — regex with pipe `|` is treated as literal pipe, **not alternation**. Should be `/message\|nhắn\|type\|input/i` → `/message|nhắn|type|input/i`. This will **never match** anything meaningful. | Line 57 |
| **HIGH** | Same pipe-regex bug on `sendButton`: `/send\|gửi\|send message/i` | Line 58 |
| **MEDIUM** | `backButton: page.locator('button:has(svg.lucide-arrow-left)')` — `:has()` CSS selector is **not natively supported** in all browsers and is deprecated in favor of Playwright's `.filter({ has })` pattern. Works in Chromium but fragile. | Line 44 |
| **MEDIUM** | Same `:has()` pattern used on `sceneButton`, `giftButton`, `emojiButton`, `micButton`, `sendGiftButton`, `closeGiftModal`, `searchButton` — **7 locators** relying on `button:has(svg.lucide-*)`. If icon library changes or SVGs are wrapped, all break. | Lines 44-48, 60-62 |
| **MEDIUM** | `passwordInput` in LoginPage: `'input[type="password"], input[placeholder*="••"]'` — the `•` bullet character may render differently across OS/browsers (macOS uses `●`, Linux `•`). Fragile. | LoginPage.ts Line 27 |
| **MEDIUM** | `usernameInput` in RegisterPage: `'input[name="username"], input[placeholder*="username" i]'` — the `i` flag in CSS selector is **not universally supported**. Works in Playwright/Chromium but not standard CSS. | RegisterPage.ts Line 25 |
| **LOW** | `pageTitle: page.locator('h1, [class*="CardTitle"]').first()` — too generic, could match any `h1` on page. | LoginPage.ts Line 33, RegisterPage.ts Line 30 |
| **LOW** | `characterName: page.locator('[data-testid="character-name"], h2:first-of-type')` — `h2:first-of-type` is extremely broad and will match wrong elements on pages with multiple h2s. | DashboardPage.ts Line 49 |
| **LOW** | `loadingSpinner: page.locator('[data-testid="loading-spinner"], .animate-spin')` — `.animate-spin` is a Tailwind utility class used on **any** spinning element (loading, icons, etc). Will cause false positives. | BasePage.ts Line 16 |

#### PasswordResetPages.ts
| Severity | Issue | Location |
|---|---|---|
| **MEDIUM** | `backToLoginLink: page.locator('a[href="/auth/login"], a:has-text("Quay lại")')` — `a:has-text()` is Playwright-proprietary, fine, but "Quay lại" means "Go back" and could match any back link, not specifically login. | Line 23 |
| **MEDIUM** | `otpInputs: page.locator('input[maxlength="1"]')` — fragile. If the app switches to a single OTP input field, this breaks entirely. | Line 80 |

---

### 2. MISSING ASSERTIONS (Tests That Always Pass)

| Severity | Test | Issue |
|---|---|---|
| **CRITICAL** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should not send empty message" | **Zero assertions**. Just checks `isDisabled` and does nothing with the result. Test always passes. | Line 94-102 |
| **CRITICAL** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should show typing indicator while waiting" | **Zero assertions**. Just sends a message and waits 500ms. No verification of typing indicator. | Lines 67-77 |
| **CRITICAL** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should search chat history" | Conditional `if (isSearchVisible)` — if search isn't implemented, test silently passes. No assertions at all. | Lines 118-127 |
| **CRITICAL** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should open gift modal" | Uses `if (!isModalVisible) { console.log(...) }` — **console.log is not an assertion**. Test always passes. | Lines 108-116 |
| **HIGH** | `password-reset.spec.ts` → "should show error for non-existent email" | Comment-only "Should show error or info message". No assertion. | Lines 28-34 |
| **HIGH** | `password-reset.spec.ts` → "should have resend OTP option" | All 4 OTP tests are **`.skip()`'d** — zero coverage. | Lines 60-94 |
| **HIGH** | `password-reset.spec.ts` → "should display password reset form" | `expect(count).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0)` — **always passes**, even with 0 password inputs. | Lines 98-104 |
| **HIGH** | `password-reset.spec.ts` → "should validate password requirements" | No assertion after submit. Just `waitForTimeout(1000)`. | Lines 106-114 |
| **HIGH** | `password-reset.spec.ts` → "should validate password match" | No assertion after submit. Just `waitForTimeout(1000)`. | Lines 116-125 |
| **HIGH** | `login.spec.ts` → "should show error for empty credentials" | `const hasError = await loginPage.isToastVisible();` — **never asserted**. Value is captured but not checked. | Lines 20-25 |
| **HIGH** | `login.spec.ts` → "should show loading state during login" | **Zero assertions**. Just clicks and waits 100ms. | Lines 82-91 |
| **MEDIUM** | `register.spec.ts` → "should show error for duplicate email" | Uses `expect(hasToast || onRegisterPage).toBeTruthy()` — logical OR means if the page stays on register (which it would for ANY error), test passes. Doesn't verify it's specifically a duplicate email error. | Lines 80-90 |
| **MEDIUM** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should handle long messages" | Only asserts URL still contains `/chat`. Doesn't verify the message was handled correctly, truncated, or rejected. | Lines 104-113 |

---

### 3. FLAKY TESTS (Timing / Race Conditions)

| Severity | Test | Issue |
|---|---|---|
| **CRITICAL** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should receive AI response" — `sendMessageAndWaitForResponse` | Uses `.catch(() => {})` to swallow errors on typing indicator waits (lines 86-89 in ChatPage.ts). If typing indicator never appears, test silently continues. Then asserts `toHaveCount(currentMessagesCount + 2)` — if AI responds at different speed or sends multiple messages, this fails randomly. **60s timeout** will cause test suite hangs. |
| **CRITICAL** | `chat.spec.ts` → "should send a message" | Uses `page.waitForTimeout(2000)` — hardcoded wait. On slow CI, 2s may not be enough; on fast machines, wastes time. |
| **HIGH** | All password-reset tests | Use `page.waitForTimeout(1000)`, `waitForTimeout(2000)`, `waitForTimeout(3000)` — **hardcoded sleeps** instead of proper waits. Fragile across environments. |
| **HIGH** | `login.spec.ts` → "should persist login state after page refresh" | After `page.reload()`, there's **no wait** before checking `isAuthenticated()`. localStorage may not be restored yet. |
| **HIGH** | `onboarding.spec.ts` → Multiple tests | Uses `page.waitForTimeout(5000)` after character creation. Arbitrary timeout that may not be enough on slow systems. |
| **HIGH** | `ChatPage.sendMessageAndWaitForResponse` | `.catch(() => {})` **swallows all errors** including real failures. Test can't distinguish "typing indicator didn't show" from "bot never responded". |
| **MEDIUM** | `register.spec.ts` → "should register successfully" | `waitForTimeout(3000)` before checking URL — may not be enough for full redirect + page load on slow CI. |
| **MEDIUM** | `onboarding.spec.ts` → "should go back to previous step" | Uses `page.waitForTimeout(500)` for animations — may not be enough on slower machines. |
| **MEDIUM** | `DashboardPage.waitForPageLoad` | `Promise.race([...]).catch(() => {})` — silently catches the case where neither character name nor create button appears. Test continues in broken state. |
| **MEDIUM** | `BasePage.waitForLoadingComplete` | `loadingSpinner.waitFor({ state: 'hidden', timeout: 10000 }).catch(() => {})` — if spinner never appears, waits full 10s unnecessarily every test. |

---

### 4. COVERAGE GAPS (Critical Flows Without Tests)

| Area | Missing Tests |
|---|---|
| **Gift System** | No UI tests for sending gifts, gift modal interactions, gift history. Only a skipped/weak `openGiftModal` test. |
| **Scene System** | No UI tests for scene selection, scene changing, scene-modal interactions. ChatPage has locators but no tests. |
| **Quest System** | No UI tests for viewing quests, claiming rewards, quest progress tracking. |
| **Memory/Gallery** | No tests for memory creation, viewing gallery, favoriting memories. |
| **Settings** | No UI tests for settings page (only a navigation check in dashboard). No tests for language, theme, notification toggles. |
| **Premium/Gating** | No tests for premium feature access, tier upgrades, payment flow. |
| **Mobile Responsive** | Only 1 mobile test (chat page). No mobile tests for auth, onboarding, dashboard, settings. |
| **Navigation/Router** | No tests for 404 pages, deep linking, URL params handling. |
| **Error States** | No tests for network errors, API failures displayed to user, offline mode. |
| **Accessibility** | No accessibility tests despite having `checkBasicAccessibility` helper (unused). |
| **WebSocket/Real-time** | No tests for real-time features (typing indicators, live messages, notifications). |
| **DM System** | No tests for direct messaging (schema has `ConversationMember`, `DirectMessage`). |
| **Streak/Daily Rewards** | No tests for daily login streak, daily reward claiming. |
| **Relationship System** | No tests for relationship stages, affection progression, level-up events. |
| **Full E2E User Journey** | No test that covers: Register → Onboarding → Chat → Send Gift → Complete Quest → View Memory in a single flow. |

---

### 5. PAGE OBJECT ISSUES

#### ChatPage.ts
| Issue | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Incorrect `fillInput` call** | `typeMessage` calls `this.fillInput(this.messageInput, message)` — `fillInput` is from BasePage and uses `locator.fill()`. This is correct, but `typeMessage` and `enterMessage` are **duplicate aliases** (DRY violation). |
| **Missing `waitForResponse` pattern** | `sendMessageAndWaitForResponse` relies on DOM counting instead of intercepting the actual API call. Should use `page.waitForResponse()` for the chat send endpoint. |
| **Gift button may not exist** | If user doesn't have gift feature unlocked, `openGiftModal` will fail with locator not found. No defensive check. |
| **`search()` method fills but doesn't trigger** | `search()` fills input but doesn't press Enter or click search button — incomplete interaction. |

#### BasePage.ts
| Issue | Detail |
|---|---|
| **`clickWithRetry` masks real bugs** | Manual retry loop (3 retries) is an anti-pattern. If a click needs retry, the underlying locator or timing is wrong. Should use Playwright's built-in auto-waiting instead. |
| **`isAuthenticated` is hardcoded to `vgfriend-auth` key** | If the app changes the localStorage key, this silently returns `false` and breaks all auth-dependent assertions. |
| **`closeToast` uses Sonner-specific selector** | `[data-sonner-toast]` assumes Sonner toast library. If toast library changes, this breaks. Should be abstracted. |
| **`loadingSpinner` selector too broad** | `.animate-spin` matches any Tailwind spinning animation, not just page-level loading spinners. |

#### LoginPage.ts
| Issue | Detail |
|---|---|
| **`showPasswordButton` selector fragile** | `page.locator('button[type="button"]').filter({ has: page.locator('svg') }).first()` — if form has any other button with an SVG (e.g., info icon), this grabs the wrong one. |

#### RegisterPage.ts

|---|---|
| **`usernameInput` visibility check in `enterUsername`** | `if (await this.usernameInput.isVisible())` — this is a sync check inside an async method. Should use `locator.isVisible()` properly. Actually it does, but it's an anti-pattern to conditionally fill — tests should explicitly test both paths. |
| **`registerButton` filter may fail** | `.filter({ hasText: /Đăng ký\|Register/i })` — same pipe regex bug as ChatPage. The `|` is a literal character, not alternation. Should be `/Đăng ký|Register/i`. |

| **`nextButton` selector too broad** | Matches any button with "Tiếp", "Next", or arrow-right SVG. Could match wrong button on multi-step forms. |

| **ResetPasswordPage `goto()` ignores params** | `goto(email, token)` accepts params but tests call `goto()` without them — tests navigate to bare `/auth/reset-password` which likely 404s or shows empty page. |
| **VerifyOTPPage `goto()` URL construction** | Appends `?email=` but app may expect different param format or use route params instead. |

response.output:
Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile the comprehensive audit report.

---

## Prisma Schema Audit Report

### 1. CRITICAL: Schema Validation Error — `UserSettings.activeSceneId` missing `@unique`

**File:** `schema.prisma:129-130`

```prisma
activeSceneId         String?
activeScene           Scene?  @relation("UserActiveScene", fields: [activeSceneId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
```

`prisma validate` **fails** with error P1012. This is a one-to-one relation (`UserSettings` ↔ `Scene` via `userActiveScene`), so the FK field `activeSceneId` **must** have `@unique`. Currently it does not.

**Fix:** Add `@unique` to `activeSceneId`:
```prisma
activeSceneId         String?  @unique
```

This is a **blocker** — `npx prisma generate` and `npx prisma db push` will both fail until this is fixed.

---

### 2. Relations with `@relation` attributes — All correct ✅

All models with relations properly define `@relation(fields: [...], references: [...])`:

| Relation | Defining Side | Fields | References | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `RefreshToken → User` | `userId` | `[userId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `UserSettings → User` | `userId` | `[userId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `UserSettings → Scene` | `activeSceneId` | `[activeSceneId]` | `[id]` | ⚠️ Missing `@unique` (see #1) |
| `Character → User` | `userId` | `[userId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `Character → CharacterTemplate` | `templateId` | `[templateId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `CharacterFact → Character` | `characterId` | `[characterId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `CharacterScene → Character` | `characterId` | `[characterId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `CharacterScene → Scene` | `sceneId` | `[sceneId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `Message → User` | `userId` | `[userId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `Message → Character` | `characterId` | `[characterId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `UserQuest → User` | `userId` | `[userId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `UserQuest → Quest` | `questId` | `[questId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `UserGift → User` | `userId` | `[userId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `UserGift → Gift` | `giftId` | `[giftId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `GiftHistory → User` | `userId` | `[userId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `GiftHistory → Character` | `characterId` | `[characterId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `GiftHistory → Gift` | `giftId` | `[giftId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `Memory → User` | `userId` | `[userId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `Memory → Character` | `characterId` | `[characterId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `ConversationSummary → User` | `userId` | `[userId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `ConversationSummary → Character` | `characterId` | `[characterId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `UserAchievement → User` | `userId` | `[userId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `UserAchievement → Achievement` | `achievementId` | `[achievementId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `Notification → User` | `userId` | `[userId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |
| `DailyReward → User` | `userId` | `[userId]` | `[id]` | ✅ |

### 5. Issue: `CharacterScene → Scene` has no explicit `onDelete`

**File:** `schema.prisma:308-309`
```prisma
character Character @relation(fields: [characterId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
scene     Scene     @relation(fields: [sceneId], references: [id])
```

`scene` relation defaults to `Restrict` — if an admin deletes a `Scene`, all `CharacterScene` rows pointing to it will **block** the deletion. This may be intentional (scenes are seeded and rarely deleted), but if scenes should be deletable, change to `onDelete: Cascade`.

---

### 6. Issue: Reference tables (`Quest`, `Gift`, `Achievement`) have no `onDelete` for join tables

`UserQuest → Quest`, `UserGift → Gift`, `GiftHistory → Gift`, `UserAchievement → Achievement` all default to `Restrict`. This means deleting a quest/gift/achievement will fail if any user has progress.

**Assessment:** This is **probably intentional** — you don't want to lose user progress when admin deletes catalog items. However, if soft-delete is desired, consider adding `isActive` flags or changing to `Cascade`.

### 11. Minor Observations (Non-blocking)

1. **`Gift` has two `@@unique` constraints**: `@@unique([name, rarity])` at line 459, and `name` is also `@unique` implicitly through `CharacterTemplate` (no — `CharacterTemplate` has its own `name @unique`). The `Gift` model does NOT have `name @unique` on the field itself, only the composite `@@unique([name, rarity])`. This is correct.

2. **`Conversation` model has no indexes** — It only has `@@map("conversations")`. Queries on `Conversation` are done through relation filters on `members`, so this is acceptable.

3. **`User.searchUsers` NOT filter** in `dm.service.ts:318-323`:
   ```typescript
   NOT: {
     settings: {
       allowMessages: false,
       profilePublic: false,
     },
   },
   ```
   This queries `User.settings` (one-to-one relation). Works correctly since `UserSettings` has `userId @unique`.

4. **`Gender` enum** has no `NOT_SPECIFIED` value (unlike `UserGender`). Characters always have a gender from `MALE, FEMALE, NON_BINARY, OTHER`. This is a design choice — acceptable.

5. **`MonitoringEventType`** enum is imported in `prisma.ts` but no service explicitly references it by name. It's used implicitly via string values in `telemetry.service.ts`. Not a bug, just dead import.

## Prisma Schema Audit Report

### 1. CRITICAL: Schema Validation Error — `UserSettings.activeSceneId` missing `@unique`

**File:** `schema.prisma:129-130`

```prisma
activeSceneId         String?
activeScene           Scene?  @relation("UserActiveScene", fields: [activeSceneId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
```

`prisma validate` **fails** with error P1012. This is a one-to-one relation (`UserSettings` ↔ `Scene` via `userActiveScene`), so the FK field `activeSceneId` **must** have `@unique`. Currently it does not.

**Fix:** Add `@unique` to `activeSceneId`:
```prisma
activeSceneId         String?  @unique
```

This is a **blocker** — `npx prisma generate` and `npx prisma db push` will both fail until this is fixed.

| `CharacterFact` | `[characterId, key]` | `characterId_key` in `character.service.ts:287`, `chat.service.ts:243`, `facts-learning.service.ts:77,169` | ✅ |
| `CharacterScene` | `[characterId, sceneId]` | `characterId_sceneId` in `scene.service.ts:249,355` | ✅ |
| `Quest` | `[title, type]` | `title_type` in `seed.ts` upsert helper | ✅ |
| `UserQuest` | `[userId, questId]` | `userId_questId` in `quest.service.ts:109,130`, `game-event.service.ts:186` | ✅ |
| `Gift` | `[name, rarity]` | `name_rarity` in `seed.ts` upsert helper | ✅ |
| `UserGift` | `[userId, giftId]` | `userId_giftId` in `gift.service.ts:88,131` | ✅ |
| `Scene` | `[name]` | Used in seed `upsertScene` via `name` | ✅ |
| `Achievement` | `[name]` | Used in seed `upsertAchievement` via `name` | ✅ |
| `UserAchievement` | `[userId, achievementId]` | Not directly referenced in code (Prisma auto-generates) | ✅ |
| `MonitoringMetricRollup` | `[bucketStart, bucketSize, metricKey, dimensionsHash]` | Not directly referenced (internal use) | ✅ |
| `ConversationMember` | `[conversationId, userId]` | DM system | ✅ |
| `CharacterTemplate` | `name` (via `@unique`) | Seed uses `name` for upsert | ✅ |
| `Gift` (additional) | `name` (via `@unique` on Scene, not Gift) | — | ✅ |

---

### 9. Enum Values — Match code usage ✅

| Enum | Schema Values | Code Usage | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `PremiumTier` | `FREE, BASIC, PRO, ULTIMATE` | Used everywhere consistently | ✅ |
| `UserGender` | `MALE, FEMALE, NON_BINARY, OTHER, NOT_SPECIFIED` | `auth.service.ts`, `gift.service.ts` | ✅ |
| `DatingPreference` | `MALE, FEMALE, NON_BINARY, ALL` | `auth.service.ts` | ✅ |
| `Gender` | `MALE, FEMALE, NON_BINARY, OTHER` | `character.service.ts` import from `@prisma/client` | ✅ |
| `RelationshipStage` | All 8 stages | `character.service.ts`, `relationship.service.ts`, `scene.service.ts` | ✅ |
| `RelationshipEventType` | All 8 event types | `relationship.service.ts` imports from `@prisma/client` | ✅ |
| `MessageRole` | `USER, AI, SYSTEM` | `gift.service.ts` uses `'SYSTEM'`, `'AI'` as strings | ✅ |
| `MessageType` | `TEXT, IMAGE, VOICE, GIFT, STICKER, EVENT` | `dm.controller.ts` Zod validates `['TEXT','IMAGE','VOICE','STICKER','GIFT']` | ✅ |
| `QuestType` | `DAILY, WEEKLY, STORY, ACHIEVEMENT, EVENT, RELATIONSHIP` | `seed.ts` uses `'DAILY'`, `'WEEKLY'`, `'STORY'`, `'ACHIEVEMENT'` | ✅ |
| `QuestStatus` | `IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CLAIMED, EXPIRED` | `quest.service.ts` checks `'IN_PROGRESS'`, `'COMPLETED'`, `'CLAIMED'` | ✅ |
| `Rarity` | `COMMON, UNCOMMON, RARE, EPIC, LEGENDARY` | `seed.ts` seeds all rarities | ✅ |
| `MemoryType` | `MILESTONE, PHOTO, CONVERSATION, GIFT, EVENT, SPECIAL, DATE, CHAT` | `memory.service.ts` uses `'MILESTONE'` | ✅ |
| `NotificationType` | `SYSTEM, CHAT, QUEST, ACHIEVEMENT, GIFT, EVENT, REWARD` | Schema only (no direct code reference found) | ✅ |
| `BillingCycle` | `MONTHLY, YEARLY` | `payment.service.ts` | ✅ |
| `SubscriptionStatus` | `ACTIVE, PAST_DUE, CANCELED, INCOMPLETE, TRIALING` | `payment.service.ts` `mapStripeStatus` maps all Stripe statuses | ✅ |
| `MonitoringEventType` | `REQUEST, ERROR, AUTH, SOCKET, JOB, SECURITY, BUSINESS` | Imported in `prisma.ts` but no usage found in services | ✅ |
| `OTPType` | `PASSWORD_RESET, REGISTRATION` | `password-reset.service.ts` | ✅ |

---

### 10. Code-to-Schema Mismatches

**No mismatches found.** All fields queried in code exist in the schema. All relation names, nested includes, and composite unique keys are consistent.

---

### 11. Minor Observations (Non-blocking)

1. **`Gift` has two `@@unique` constraints**: `@@unique([name, rarity])` at line 459, and `name` is also `@unique` implicitly through `CharacterTemplate` (no — `CharacterTemplate` has its own `name @unique`). The `Gift` model does NOT have `name @unique` on the field itself, only the composite `@@unique([name, rarity])`. This is correct.

2. **`Conversation` model has no indexes** — It only has `@@map("conversations")`. Queries on `Conversation` are done through relation filters on `members`, so this is acceptable.

3. **`User.searchUsers` NOT filter** in `dm.service.ts:318-323`:
   ```typescript
   NOT: {
     settings: {
       allowMessages: false,
       profilePublic: false,
     },
   },
   ```
   This queries `User.settings` (one-to-one relation). Works correctly since `UserSettings` has `userId @unique`.

4. **`Gender` enum** has no `NOT_SPECIFIED` value (unlike `UserGender`). Characters always have a gender from `MALE, FEMALE, NON_BINARY, OTHER`. This is a design choice — acceptable.

5. **`MonitoringEventType`** enum is imported in `prisma.ts` but no service explicitly references it by name. It's used implicitly via string values in `telemetry.service.ts`. Not a bug, just dead import.

---

### Summary of Issues

| # | Severity | Issue | Location |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **🔴 CRITICAL** | `UserSettings.activeSceneId` missing `@unique`, blocking `prisma validate` | `schema.prisma:129` |
| 2 | 🟡 Medium | `CharacterScene → Scene` has no `onDelete` (defaults to Restrict) | `schema.prisma:309` |
| 3 | 🟡 Low | Reference tables (`Quest`, `Gift`, `Achievement`) join tables default to Restrict — may block admin deletes | `schema.prisma:413, 483, 502, 637` |
| 4 | 🟡 Low | `Conversation` model has no indexes | `schema.prisma:827-837` |
| 5 | ℹ️ Info | `MonitoringEventType` imported but never used by name | `prisma.ts:1` |

**Unresolved questions:** None.
